step_by_step_thinking:
Prevalence of pit & fissure caries is best measured with **International Caries Detection and Assessment System (ICDAS)**, given its capability to accurately detect noncavitated lesions, although it requires additional time investment and depends on operator's skill level.
\boxed{A} Based on the detailed review of the literature, the International Caries Detection and Assessment System (ICDAS) is specifically noted for its accuracy in identifying noncavitated lesions in pits and fissures. While it takes longer to apply compared to simpler indices like DMF, its precision makes it highly suitable for assessing the prevalence of pit and fissure caries.
